---
title: "ARIMA fDOM Forecasting — No-Refit Workflow (Fixed Coefficients)"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 2
    theme: flatly
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---


## Packages and Data

Uses `fable::ARIMA()` with automatic order selection (AIC-based), fit **once** on
pre-2024 training data. Univariate models fit a standard ARIMA; covariate models fit
regression with ARIMA errors (ARIMAX), following Melofton et al.

Forecasting uses `fabletools::refit(..., reestimate = FALSE)` to update the model
**state** (residuals, differenced values) to the reference date **without re-estimating
any parameters** — the AR/MA/regression coefficients selected on pre-2024 data are
reused for every one of the ~730 reference dates. This is the same no-refit design
used across the AR, NNETAR, and XGBoost `_NoRefit` scripts.

```{r packages}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(fable)      # ARIMA()
library(fabletools) # refit()
library(feasts)     # gg_tsresiduals()
library(tsibble)
library(zoo)        # na.approx
```

```{r load-data}
getwd()
ar_df <- read_csv("../Data/Daily_catwalk_RH_2021_2026.csv") |>
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date)) |>
  select(-fDOM_1m_lag1)   # lag column not needed — ARIMA handles AR lags internally


# ar_df |> ggplot(aes(x = Date, y = fDOM_1_QSU_daily))+ geom_point()

# Training window: all data before 2024
train_cutoff <- ymd("2024-01-01")

# Prediction window: every day of 2024 and 2025
pred_dates <- seq(ymd("2024-01-01"), ymd("2025-12-31"), by = "day")

```

```{r interpolate-short-gaps}
# Interpolate short gaps only (≤ 10 consecutive missing days) in target and all drivers.
# Gaps longer than 10 days are left as NA — those reference dates will be skipped
ar_df <- ar_df |>
  mutate(across(-Date,
                \(x) zoo::na.approx(x, maxgap = 10, na.rm = FALSE, rule = 2)))

```

---

## Model Fitting

### Fitting function

Fits an ARIMA model via `fable::ARIMA()` on data before `train_cutoff`. Order (p,d,q)
is selected automatically by AIC. With covariates, fits regression with ARIMA errors
(ARIMAX) — identical to Melofton et al.'s `(b) Reg. w/ ARIMA errors` specification.
This is the only time the order and coefficients are estimated.

Returns the fitted mable, training plot, residual diagnostics, and metadata.

```{r fn-fit}
fit_arima_model <- function(all_data,
                             train_cutoff,
                             target,
                             covariates  = NULL,
                             model_name,
                             seed        = 100) {

  set.seed(seed)

  col_select <- c(target, covariates)

  train_df <- all_data |>
    filter(Date < train_cutoff) |>
    select(Date, all_of(col_select)) |>
    arrange(Date)

  train_ts <- as_tsibble(train_df, index = Date)

  message(model_name, " | n_train = ", nrow(train_df))

  # Fit ARIMA — univariate uses sym injection; covariate uses standard formula
  if (is.null(covariates)) {
    arima_fit <- train_ts |>
      model(arima = fable::ARIMA(!!sym(target)))
  } else {
    formula_obj <- as.formula(paste(target, "~",
                                    paste(covariates, collapse = " + ")))
    arima_fit <- train_ts |>
      model(arima = fable::ARIMA(formula = formula_obj))
  }

  # Report selected ARIMA order — model_sum() gives the compact structure
  # (e.g. "LM w/ ARIMA(2,0,3) errors"), unlike report()'s first line which is
  # just the series name
  model_structure <- fabletools::model_sum(arima_fit$arima[[1]])
  message(model_name, " | model structure: ", model_structure)

  # In-sample fitted values
  fitted_vals <- fitted(arima_fit) |>
    as_tibble() |>
    select(Date, .fitted)

  train_df_plot <- train_df |>
    left_join(fitted_vals, by = "Date")

  train_rmse <- sqrt(mean((train_df_plot[[target]] - train_df_plot$.fitted)^2,
                           na.rm = TRUE))

  # Training plot: observed vs fitted timeseries
  p_fit <- ggplot(train_df_plot) +
    geom_point(aes(x = Date, y = !!sym(target), color = "Observed"),
               size = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
    geom_point(aes(x = Date, y = .fitted, color = "ARIMA fitted"),
               size = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Observed" = "black", "ARIMA fitted" = "dodgerblue"),
                       breaks = c("Observed", "ARIMA fitted")) +
    labs(title = paste0(model_name, " — training fit (pre-", year(train_cutoff), ")",
                        "  |  RMSE = ", round(train_rmse, 2), " QSU"),
         x = "Date", y = "fDOM (QSU)", color = NULL) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(legend.position = "top")

  # Residual diagnostics (Melofton et al. approach)
  p_resid <- gg_tsresiduals(arima_fit)

  list(
    model            = arima_fit,
    plot             = p_fit,
    resid_plot       = p_resid,
    model_name       = model_name,
    model_structure  = model_structure,
    target           = target,
    covariates       = covariates,
    train_rmse       = train_rmse
  )
}
```

### Fit 3 models

All models trained on data before `r train_cutoff`. ARIMA orders and coefficients are
fixed here and reused for all 2024-2025 forecasts via `refit(reestimate = FALSE)`.

```{r fit-models}
# ARIMA1: univariate (auto-selected order, no external regressors)
# ARIMA_lagonly <- fit_arima_model(
#   all_data     = ar_df,
#   train_cutoff = train_cutoff,
#   target       = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily",
#   covariates   = NULL,
#   model_name   = "ARIMA_lag_only"
# )

# ARIMA2: regression w/ ARIMA errors + in-lake (Temp, DO, Chla)
ARIMA_lake <- fit_arima_model(
  all_data     = ar_df,
  train_cutoff = train_cutoff,
  target       = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily",
  covariates   = c("Diff_Dens_1_max", "DOsat_1_pct_daily", "Chla_1_ugL_daily"),
  model_name   = "ARIMA_lag_lake"
)

# ARIMA3: regression w/ ARIMA errors + catchment (RH discharge and DOC)
ARIMA_catch <- fit_arima_model(
  all_data     = ar_df,
  train_cutoff = train_cutoff,
  target       = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily",
  covariates   = c("RH_Q_cms", "RH_DOC_mgL"),
  model_name   = "ARIMA_lag_catchment"
)

# ARIMA4: regression w/ ARIMA errors + in-lake + catchment
ARIMA_lake_catch <- fit_arima_model(
  all_data     = ar_df,
  train_cutoff = train_cutoff,
  target       = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily",
  covariates   = c("Diff_Dens_1_max", "DOsat_1_pct_daily", "Chla_1_ugL_daily",
                   "RH_Q_cms", "RH_DOC_mgL"),
  model_name   = "ARIMA_lag_lake_catchment"
)
```

### Training plots

```{r plot-training, fig.height=4}
ARIMA_lake$plot
ARIMA_catch$plot
ARIMA_lake_catch$plot
```


### model structure

Selected ARIMA structure for each model:

```{r print-structure}
tibble(
  model_name      = c(ARIMA_lake$model_name, ARIMA_catch$model_name, ARIMA_lake_catch$model_name),
  model_structure = c(ARIMA_lake$model_structure, ARIMA_catch$model_structure, ARIMA_lake_catch$model_structure)
)
```

---

## 30-Day Forecast Function

`refit(..., reestimate = FALSE)` feeds the observed history up to the reference date
through the fixed ARIMA model to update the internal state (last residuals, differenced
values) only — this is the initial-condition update, not a refit. Parameters (AR/MA
coefficients, regression coefficients) are unchanged from the single pre-2024 fit.

`forecast()` then projects 30 days forward. For covariate models, observed covariate
values for the forecast period are supplied via `new_data` (perfect covariate assumption).

```{r fn-forecast}
# # inputs to test function
# forecast_date <- ymd("2025-01-01")
# model_obj <- ARIMA_lake_catch
# all_data <- ar_df
# target  = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily"
# horizon = 30

forecast_fdom_30day <- function(forecast_date,
                                 model_obj,
                                 all_data,
                                 target  = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily",
                                 horizon = 30) {

  covariates <- model_obj$covariates
  col_select <- c(target, covariates)

  # Check observed fDOM exists on reference date
  curr_fdom <- all_data |>
    filter(Date == forecast_date) |>
    pull(!!sym(target))

  if (length(curr_fdom) == 0 || is.na(curr_fdom)) return(NULL)

  # Full observed history up to and including reference date
  # (used only to update ARIMA state — target + covariates both needed)
  history_ts <- all_data |>
    filter(Date <= forecast_date) |>
    select(Date, all_of(col_select)) |>
    arrange(Date) |>
    as_tsibble(index = Date)

  # Update state with fixed parameters (no re-estimation)
  model_updated <- model_obj$model |>
    refit(new_data = history_ts, reestimate = FALSE)

  # Forecast dates: day 1 through day 30 after reference date
  forecast_dates <- seq(forecast_date + 1, forecast_date + horizon, by = "day")

  # Generate forecast
  if (!is.null(covariates)) {
    # Provide observed covariate values for forecast period (perfect covariate assumption)
    future_ts <- all_data |>
      filter(Date %in% forecast_dates) |>
      select(Date, all_of(covariates)) |>
      arrange(Date) |>
      as_tsibble(index = Date)

    if (nrow(future_ts) == 0) return(NULL)

    fc <- forecast(model_updated, new_data = future_ts)
  } else {
    fc <- forecast(model_updated, h = horizon)
  }

  # Extract mean forecast and format to match all other model outputs
  fc |>
    as_tibble() |>
    select(Date, .mean) |>
    mutate(
      model_id          = model_obj$model_name,
      reference_date    = forecast_date,
      Horizon           = as.integer(Date - reference_date),
      fDOM_1m_predicted = .mean
    ) |>
    select(model_id, reference_date, forecast_date = Date, Horizon, fDOM_1m_predicted) |>
    filter(Horizon > 0)
}

# # test function
# zz <- forecast_fdom_30day(forecast_date = ymd("2025-01-01"), model_obj = ARIMA_lake_catch,
#                     all_data = ar_df, target = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily", horizon = 30)

```

---

## Run Forecasts Across 2024-2025

```{r fn-loop}
run_forecasts <- function(model_obj,
                           all_data,
                           pred_dates,
                           target  = "fDOM_1_QSU_daily",
                           horizon = 30) {

  valid_dates <- all_data |>
    filter(Date %in% pred_dates, !is.na(!!sym(target))) |>
    pull(Date)

  message(model_obj$model_name, " | forecasting ", length(valid_dates),
          " reference dates...")

  map_dfr(valid_dates, \(d) {
    message("  -> starting forecast for ", d)
    forecast_fdom_30day(
      forecast_date = d,
      model_obj     = model_obj,
      all_data      = all_data,
      target        = target,
      horizon       = horizon
    )
  })
}
```


```{r run-forecasts}
#forecasts_arima1 <- run_forecasts(ARIMA_lagonly,    ar_df, pred_dates)
forecasts_arima2 <- run_forecasts(ARIMA_lake,       ar_df, pred_dates)
forecasts_arima3 <- run_forecasts(ARIMA_catch,      ar_df, pred_dates)
forecasts_arima4 <- run_forecasts(ARIMA_lake_catch, ar_df, pred_dates)

all_ARIMA_forecasts <- bind_rows(forecasts_arima2, forecasts_arima3, forecasts_arima4)

glimpse(all_ARIMA_forecasts)
```

---

## Save Output

```{r save}
write_csv(all_ARIMA_forecasts,
          "../Data/ARIMA_forecasts_2024-2025_NoRefit_17july26.csv")

```
